$comment: Creature-state thresholds on the SMOOTHED burn rate (tokens/min). The signal includes cache by default (settings.rateCacheWeight=1.0), so it runs ~70x larger than work-only; these cutoffs are calibrated for that cache-inclusive scale (avg cache ~118k/turn measured). If you set rateCacheWeight=0 (work only), divide these by ~75 (working ~1500, stressed ~8000, onfire ~25000). Each state has separate up/down cutoffs (hysteresis) so a noisy signal does not strobe. Tune freely — read live in dev, no rebuild needed.

sleeping.$note: entered when no active block; not rate-driven

onfire.$note: onfire requires the high rate to be sustained, not a single spike

spent.$note: spent = the crash AFTER burning hot: when the rate collapses below rateThreshold within afterOnfireSeconds of being onfire, the rat is spent for durationSeconds (then relaxes to calm). NOT tied to quota exhaustion.

spike.$note: instant-rate jump (tokens/min) above this triggers a transient flinch bounce (cache-inclusive scale; ~80k on work-only)

quota.$note: Layer 2 — near-limit overlay ramp. The overlay opacity rises linearly from 0 to 1 as consumedWithCache/ceiling climbs from startPercent to fullPercent (the readout shows a % in this band, then a refresh countdown at/over the ceiling). The ceiling auto-calibrates to your largest recent completed block (see settings.default.json limitHistoryDays / limitMinCredibleTokens; planLimits can override it).

events.$note: Layer 3 — transient one-shot events. `priority` resolves which single event plays when several fire at once (highest first). The cooldowns keep a retryable hiccup from spamming an event; flinch is gated by the spike detector so it needs none.

$activityFloorNote: Fast-attack perk-up: if a token was written within this many seconds, the rat is at least 'working' immediately (don't wait for the smoothed rate to climb). Higher states still come from the smoothed rate.
$idleTimeoutNote: Smart napping: the nap clock runs from the last conversational line (user or assistant), so a new message resets it; 'done' (an open question / finished turn) never naps until done_hold elapses. This is the hold time (seconds) after the last activity before the rat sleeps.
$doneHoldNote: How long to hold the 'done' pose after a finished turn before the rat naps. 120 = 2 minutes.
$sentNote: While we're awaiting Claude's reply to a message we sent (Awaiting::Sent), the rat holds the 'thinking' pose and never naps — the wait ends the instant Claude responds. No timeout knob: not napping mid-wait is the whole point (Sleep Bug fix).
$hookSignalTtlNote: Lifecycle-hook fusion (#1): when the opt-in hook bridge is connected, a hook edge (Stop/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse) refines the discrete state instantly instead of waiting on the JSONL tail. The more-recent of (hook, JSONL) wins; this TTL is a backstop so a stale hook can't override JSONL forever (e.g. after the app missed events). No effect when the bridge is disabled.
